mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, systematically compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of rising rate periods over a 3-year walk-forward evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative equity repricing as investors anticipate weaker consumer spending.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and deceleration in core inflation indicates a supply-side driven expansion that supports corporate margins, generating sustained positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average during a monetary tightening cycle confirms weakening aggregate supply, driving negative forward equity returns as growth expectations fall.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of tightening regime observations over a 4-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real stock prices reflect increased household wealth and consumption capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings expectations and generates positive short-term equity momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of high RSAFS periods over a 3-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rate indicates labor force participation expansion outpacing job creation, signaling underlying labor market slack that suppresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: Divergence signal correlates positively with 20-day returns or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 4.0 percent marks a restrictive monetary policy stance that systematically reduces equity risk premiums and produces negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are positive in >50% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts below 1.2 million units during an economic expansion signals weakening consumer confidence and reduced credit demand, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of late-cycle expansion observations over a 4-year walk-forward test.
